title: rubus culminicola
intro: Rubus culminicola, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of shrub native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to expel intestinal worms and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Rubus culminicola include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to reduce fever and improve respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Rubus culminicola often include the roots, leaves, and stems, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
